title: Record stockpile of sanctioned crude builds in Shandong amid supply disruption fears
section_path: Record stockpile of sanctioned crude builds in Shandong amid supply disruption fears
section_path_text: Record stockpile of sanctioned crude builds in Shandong amid supply disruption fears
summary:
Main
Chinese teapot refiners ramped up sanctioned crude imports in March, pushing Shandong's crude stockpiles to record highs amid intensifying US sanctions
By Emma Li
China's seaborne crude imports rebounded to 10.6mbd in March-the highest since October 2023-driven largely by record Iranian crude arrivals into the Shandong region. The latest OFAC sanctions targeting additional Iran-linked tankers have deepened concerns about potential disruptions to Iranian crude flows, prompting refiners to accelerate stockpiling of this critical feedstock. Iranian crude imports into China surged to a record 1.8mbd in March, with Shandong alone absorbing over 1.5mbd and marking a nearly 50% jump from the 2024 average.
